title: Firms expand Nigeria’s real estate frontier with launch of Real Estate Today TV programme
description: Firms expand Nigeria’s real estate frontier with launch of Real Estate Today TV programme  The Guardian Nigeria News
content: Firms expand Nigeria’s real estate frontier with launch of Real Estate Today TV programme By : Bertram Nwannekanma Date: 4 July 2026 10:05am WAT Share : Follow Us on Google Discover A real estate urban development project. A real estate urban development project. To promote transparency and boost investment in Nigeria’s property sector, M.I. Okoro & Associates and QShelter Ltd have launched Real Estate Today, a weekly television magazine programme that will air on NTA Network, Arise Television, Channels TV and TVC. Speaking at the launch in Lagos, the Founding Partner of M.I. Okoro & Associates, Mr Meckson Innocent Okoro, said the initiative followed the successful partnership between the two firms, formalised through a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) signed on December 30, 2025, for the marketing of major housing projects, including Renewed Hope Estate in Karsana, Abuja; Janguza Estate in Kano; and the 2004 Estate along the Lekki Coastal Road, Lagos. According to him, sustained interaction with the public revealed a major gap in access to reliable information on real estate and environmental issues, particularly on television. He noted that while newspapers devote space to property matters, there has been no dedicated television platform providing consistent education and authoritative information on the sector. “That is why M.I. Okoro & Associates and QShelter Ltd considered it necessary to launch Real Estate Today on television in its first phase. It will later be expanded to radio as a weekly programme covering real estate and environmental issues. “We see this selfless project as part of our Corporate Social Responsibility to support government efforts in bridging the information gap in the real estate and environmental sectors. Real Estate Today will catalyze informed discussions on housing and environmental issues in Nigeria,” Okoro said. He stressed the need for greater market transparency, investor education and structured information to help address Nigeria housing deficit . Okoro explained that the programme is designed to provide viewers with practical, reliable and up-to-date information on property investment, housing development, land acquisition, documentation and property management. He observed that although real estate remains one of the country’s strongest wealth-creation sectors, many Nigerians still lack adequate knowledge about buying property, verifying land titles, avoiding fraudulent transactions and identifying genuine investment opportunities. According to him, Real Estate Today will bridge this knowledge gap by bringing together industry professionals and experts through interviews, panel discussions, field reports and property showcases. The programme will also educate viewers on land acquisition procedures, property documentation—including Certificates of Occupancy, Deeds of Assignment and Governor’s Consent—new housing developments, investment opportunities, government policies and measures for avoiding fraudulen
summary: M.I. Okoro & Associates and QShelter Ltd launched Real Estate Today, a weekly TV programme on NTA Network, Arise Television, Channels TV, and TVC to promote transparency and boost investment in Nigeria's property sector. This initiative aims to address the information gap in the real estate and environmental sectors, with a focus on housing and environmental issues. The programme will initially air on TV and later expand to radio. This development is positive for the Lagos market, particularly for developers like Mixta Africa, as it may increase investor confidence and demand for housing projects.

title: Lagos Court Dismisses Customer’s N1.12bn Claim Against Property Firm
description: Lagos Court Dismisses Customer’s N1.12bn Claim Against Property Firm  LEADERSHIP Newspapers
content: A Lagos State High Court has dismissed a N1.12 billion counterclaim filed against a real estate developer, Oak Homes Multinational Services Limited, in a long-running dispute over a property transaction, ruling that the matter was a civil contractual disagreement rather than a case of fraud. The judgment, delivered by Justice A.A. George on June 15, 2026, in Suit No. LD/4471LM/2023, resolved a dispute between the property firm and a customer, Mr. Anthony Ehiedu Ugbebor, over the purchase of two three-bedroom apartments at Oak Residences in Victoria Island, Lagos. According to a statement issued by Oak Homes, the dispute arose after delays in the project, which the company attributed to force majeure events, including the COVID-19 pandemic. The firm also maintained that the customer completed payment of N152 million out of the N190 million purchase price only in June 2022, contrary to the agreed payment schedule. Rather than continue negotiations, Ugbebor petitioned the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) in October 2022, alleging fraud, obtaining by false pretence and criminal breach of trust against the company and its management. After reviewing documentary evidence and witness testimonies, Justice George held that the conduct of both parties had substantially altered the original agreement, amounting to a legal novation of the contract. The court consequently dismissed the customer’s counterclaims, including requests for specific performance, N20 million in special damages, N10 million in general damages, N5 million as legal fees, and an alternative claim of N1.12 billion, representing the alleged market value of comparable apartments, alongside 20 per cent annual interest from 2017. The court declared that the original contract had been terminated under the doctrine of novation and ordered the EFCC to return to Ugbebor all funds paid to it by Oak Homes through manager’s cheques. It also directed Oak Homes to remit the balance of payments previously made by the claimant, while making no order as to costs. The judgment further observed that the EFCC should not be used as a debt recovery agency in resolving civil contractual disputes, a position Oak Homes described as having wider implications for commercial transactions in Nigeria. Reacting to the ruling, the company said the judgment vindicated its long-held position that it had not defrauded any customer. “For years, Oak Homes and its Chief Executive, Mr. Olukayode Olusanya, have been the target of a deliberate and sustained campaign of reputational attacks, sponsored and amplified across internet platforms and social media,” the company said. RELATED NEWS Foreign Investors’ NGX Transactions Drop 25.9% To ₦183.61bn In May European Bank for Reconstruction Eyes Nigerian Banks to Drive Investment Push BudgIT Seeks Probe Of N1.3bn PFIPC Budget Allocation “Our company made a conscious and principled decision not to respond in kind. We chose to trust the justice system to speak. It has
summary: A Lagos State High Court dismissed a N1.12 billion counterclaim against Oak Homes Multinational Services Limited, ruling that the matter was a civil contractual disagreement rather than a case of fraud. This outcome suggests that Lagos courts are increasingly favoring developers in disputes, potentially reducing the risk of costly litigation for property firms. The case does not directly impact Mixta's live projects, but it may contribute to a more favorable business environment for real estate developers in Lagos.
trending_topics: Lagos real estate market, property disputes, court rulings

title: Group Hails Anti-Graft Agency, Seeks Probe Into Abuja Property Allegations
description: Group Hails Anti-Graft Agency, Seeks Probe Into Abuja Property Allegations  LEADERSHIP Newspapers
content: A group of lawyers and the civil society organisation, Initiative Against Human Rights Abuse and Torture (INAHURAT), has commended the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) for intervention on enforcing a Federal High Court interim forfeiture order on a property located in Guzape, Abuja. The group made the commendation yesterday during a press conference in Abuja addressed by its legal representative, Dr Maxwell Opara. Speaking at the briefing, Opara expressed concern over incidents that reportedly occurred during the execution of the court order and called on the Nigeria Police Force to investigate the circumstances surrounding the operation. According to him, the group received reports that there was resistance during the enforcement of the court’s interim forfeiture order, stressing the need for a thorough and impartial investigation. “We are calling on the Inspector-General of Police to immediately investigate who deployed those officers to the property and under whose authority they acted. “We call on the current Inspector-General of Police to immediately order a comprehensive investigation and address Nigerians on this matter,” he said. Opara also urged relevant authorities to ensure that court orders are respected and enforced in line with the provisions of the law, describing adherence to judicial decisions as fundamental to the rule of law. He commended the EFCC for carrying out the court order, saying the anti-graft agency had demonstrated commitment to upholding justice. RELATED NEWS When Breakup Is Not An Option Sokoto, Kebbi Strengthen Ties As Gov Idris Commissions Road In Tambuwal NCDC Partners Kano Centre On Local Research “We commend public officials when they do the right thing, and we condemn them when they do otherwise. The EFCC Chairman has demonstrated courage by ensuring that justice is done and the rule of law is upheld,” he stated. The group noted that the property remains the subject of ongoing legal proceedings and expressed confidence that all parties involved would continue to observe due process throughout the judicial process. INAHURAT further called on the Nigeria Immigration Service and the Ministry of Interior to clarify issues relating to passport issuance that were raised during the briefing. It also appealed to the Attorney-General of the Federation and Minister of Justice, Lateef Fagbemi, to ensure that all legal processes connected to the matter are handled strictly in accordance with the law. title: Housevisory launches AI-powered property platform to transform Nigeria’s real estate market
description: Housevisory launches AI-powered property platform to transform Nigeria’s real estate market  The Guardian Nigeria News
content: Housevisory launches AI-powered property platform to transform Nigeria’s real estate market By : Sulaimon Salau Date: 4 July 2026 4:00am WAT Share : Follow Us on Google Discover Affordable housing Affordable housing Nigeria’s journey towards a more transparent, technology-driven, and accessible real estate sector reached a significant milestone with the official launch of Housevisory, an innovative property technology platform designed to simplify how Nigerians discover, compare, verify, and secure property opportunities. The event featured a keynote address by Brigadier General GU Nwamba, Deputy Chief of Civil-Military Affairs (Psychological Operations), Army Headquarters, who underscored the importance of trust, transparency, and collaboration in national development. According to Nwamba, technology has become a critical driver of social and economic transformation, and platforms such as Housevisory have an important role to play in strengthening confidence within Nigeria’s housing ecosystem. Delivering the opening remarks, Mrs. Lynda Odunmbaku welcomed participants and described the launch as more than the introduction of a digital platform but a movement toward a smarter, more transparent property market. She noted that while housing remains one of the most important aspirations for millions of Nigerians, accessing reliable property information continues to be a major challenge for buyers, renters, developers, and investors alike. Speaking at the launch, Founder and Head of Product for Housevisory, Mr. Mathew Nnang, described the unveiling of the platform as the culmination of a vision to modernize Nigeria’s property market by leveraging technology that meets global standards while addressing local realities. According to Nnang, Housevisory was created to bridge the gap between traditional real estate practices and the rapidly evolving global PropTech landscape, providing Nigerians with a smarter, more transparent, and user-centric way to search for, compare, verify, and secure property. “Our vision is to position Nigerian property technology on par with emerging global trends by building a platform that combines artificial intelligence, data intelligence, and digital innovation to deliver a seamless property experience. We believe Nigerians deserve the same level of convenience, transparency, and confidence that property seekers enjoy in the world’s leading digital real estate markets.” Nnang added that the company’s long-term ambition is to become Nigeria’s most trusted property technology platform. Delivering a goodwill message, Ms. Toyin Gbajabiamila congratulated the Housevisory team for introducing a solution that responds to a genuine market need. Tags: Digital innovation innovative property technology transparent property market
summary: The article reports the launch of Housevisory, an AI-powered property platform, aiming to transform Nigeria's real estate market by providing a smarter and more transparent way to discover, compare, and secure property opportunities, which could increase competition in the Lagos market. The launch event highlighted the importance of trust, transparency, and collaboration in national development, and the platform's potential to strengthen confidence in Nigeria's housing ecosystem. This development may impact the Lagos real estate market, particularly in the affordable housing segment.
trending_topics: PropTech,affordable housing,real estate innovation

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: Increased competition in the Lagos real estate market may affect Mixta's market share, particularly in the affordable housing segment, such as Lakowe Crossings and Lagos New Town.

title: Experts advocate investment in real estate as firm marks fourth anniversary
description: Experts advocate investment in real estate as firm marks fourth anniversary  The Guardian Nigeria News
content: Experts advocate investment in real estate as firm marks fourth anniversary By : Opeyemi Babalola Date: 4 July 2026 4:00am WAT Share : Follow Us on Google Discover Abode’s Fourth Anniversary Conference Abode’s Fourth Anniversary Conference Amid the astronomical rise in house rent across major Nigerian cities, citizens have been urged to invest in real estate and financial literacy as essential tools for building long-term wealth. Real estate experts made the remarks during Abode’s Fourth Anniversary Conference and the graduation ceremony of Cohort VI of its Advanced Realtor Certification Programme. Opening the conference, which recorded more than 3,000 registrations, underscoring the growing interest in professional real estate education; the Chief Executive Officer of Abode, Jeffrey Itepu, reflected on the company’s four-year journey, describing its mission as one centred on shared prosperity through accessible real estate and simplified asset ownership. He noted that while the organisation had grown significantly since inception, its commitment to helping more Nigerians build wealth through property investment remained unchanged. Itepu also encouraged participants to recognise the enduring value of real estate, noting that the increasing interest in land investment is driven by its long-term potential for wealth creation and financial security. “If people are investing in land, there must be a reason for it,” he said, urging attendees to look beyond short-term trends and understand the lasting value of property ownership as a pathway to building sustainable wealth. Speaking during the keynote sessions, Mr. Ebunoluwa Olufemi emphasised the importance of knowledge in navigating today’s real estate market, encouraging participants to continuously improve their understanding of the industry. According to him, real estate education remains one of the strongest foundations for making smart investment decisions, identifying opportunities and helping clients invest with confidence. Ebunoluwa challenged participants to redefine success in the industry, urging them to look beyond commissions and focus on ownership. “Your gain is not just money but also ownership,” he said. The event brought together industry leaders, investors, professionals and aspiring realtors for a day of learning, reflection and celebration. It featured a presentation by Abode’s Chief Operating Officer, Damilare Oshokoya, who outlined the company’s strategic direction and reaffirmed its commitment to making asset ownership more accessible through innovation, transparency and education. He noted that the firm’s vision extends beyond helping people acquire land or own property, explaining that the company’s focus is on creating opportunities for individuals and families to achieve financial security, build sustainable wealth and participate meaningfully in Nigeria’s growing real estate sector. “Big dreams can start from small places,” he said, describing the firm’s growth as proof tha
summary: Experts at Abode's Fourth Anniversary Conference in Lagos advocate for investment in real estate, citing its long-term potential for wealth creation and financial security. This trend is driven by the increasing interest in land investment, with over 3,000 registrations for the conference. The advice is relevant to Lagos real estate, where house rent has been rising astronomically.
trending_topics: real estate investment,land investment,financial literacy

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: The trend of increasing interest in land investment may positively impact Mixta's position in the Lagos market, particularly for projects like Lakowe Crossings and Lakowe Annexe.

title: EFCC arraigns siblings for alleged land fraud
description: EFCC arraigns siblings for alleged land fraud  Punch Newspapers
content: EFCC arraigns siblings for alleged land fraud July 4, 2026 1:12 am Cypril Nwalaezi, and Smart Nwalaezi. Credit: EFCC By Victor Ayeni Kindly share this story: Two brothers, Cypril Nwalaezi, and Smart Nwalaezi, have been arraigned by the Port Harcourt Zonal Directorate of the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission before Justice S. D. Pam of the Federal High Court, Port Harcourt for property fraud. According to a statement posted on the EFCC’s X handle on Friday, the duo was arraigned on Thursday on an amended five-count charge, bordering on conspiracy and obtaining money under false pretense to the tune of N3,200,000. Count two read, “That you, Cypril Nwalaezi on or about the 17th October, 2022 at Port Harcourt, within the jurisdiction of this Honourable Court, with intent to defraud and by false pretence that you had valid and subsisting title to a parcel of land, situated at Umuasukpo Farm Road, in Igbo-Etche in Etche Local Government Area of Rivers State, measuring 1150.805 and 1295.771 square metres. “You also induced Mr Franklin Nwachukwu to purchase the same and obtained the sum of N2,750,000.00) from Mr Franklin Nwachukwu and thereby committed an offence contrary to Section 1(1)(b) & (c) of the Advance Fee Fraud and Other Fraud Related Offenses Act, 2006 and punishable under Section 1(3) of the same Act.” Another counts charge read, “That you, Smart Chinedu Nwalaezi on or about the third day of November, 2022 at Port Harcourt, within the jurisdiction of this Honourable Court, with intent to defraud and by false pretense that you had valid and subsisting title to a parcel of land, situated at Umuasukpo Farm Road, lgbo-Etche in Etche Local Government Area of Rivers State, measuring an area of 467.912 sq/m, induced Mr. Franklin Nwachukwu to purchase the same and obtained the sum of N450,000 from Mr Franklin Nwachukwu and thereby committed an offence contrary to Section 1(1)(b) & (c )of the Advance Fee Fraud and Other Fraud Related Offenses Act, 2006 and punishable under Section 1(3) of the same Act.” Pensioners hail Tinubu over N39.6bn arrears payment Peter Obi warns of worsening hunger crisis in northern Nigeria Rescued from the streets: Former area boys recount journeys from despair to purpose Both Cyril and Smart pleaded “not guilty,” following which prosecution counsel, C. Okorie, asked the court for a trial date and for the court to remand them in the custody of the Nigeria Correctional Service, Port Harcourt, Rivers State, while the counsel to the first defendant, R. O. Agbabo informed the court of her subsisting bail application for her client and prayed the court to grant him bail to enable him prepare for his trial. Similarly, counsel to the second defendant, M. M. Nweke, informed the court of his bail application for his client and pleaded with the court to grant him bail on liberal terms to enable him to prepare for his trial. “Justice Pam granted bail to Cypril Nwalaezi, the first defendant in the sum of N500,000 and a surety in
summary: The article reports on the arraignment of two siblings for alleged land fraud in Port Harcourt, involving a total of N3,200,000. This case highlights the issue of land fraud in Nigeria, which can impact the real estate market in Lagos by reducing investor confidence and increasing the need for thorough due diligence. The Lagos market may see a low to medium impact, with a potential increase in scrutiny of land transactions.

title: Abuja developer pushes quality, compliance amid rising housing demand
description: Abuja developer pushes quality, compliance amid rising housing demand  Vanguard News
content: As demand for organised housing continues to grow in Nigeria’s capital, the founder of Festrut Group, Professor Festus Uwakhemen Asikhia, says the company is prioritising professionally managed residential estates built on quality, transparency and regulatory compliance. Africans& Diaspora Asikhia, who serves as the Chief Executive Officer of the real estate firm, said the company’s growth has been driven by an emphasis on proper documentation, responsible estate management and investor confidence. Before venturing into real estate, Asikhia worked with the Nigerian Security and Civil Defence Corps, where he said he developed skills in planning, accountability and operational management that later influenced his approach to business. Africans& Diaspora He established Festrut Global Shelter Nigeria Limited in 2010 with the aim of developing residential communities that combine quality infrastructure with effective management and secure property documentation. Over the years, the company has delivered housing projects in parts of the Federal Capital Territory, including Defender’s Estate in Karshi, Festrut Kingdom Estate in Orozo and Festrut Estate in Katampe. According to the company, its operations place emphasis on compliance with regulatory requirements, title documentation and customer service—areas that have become increasingly important as concerns over land ownership and property transactions persist within Nigeria’s real estate sector. Industry stakeholders have repeatedly identified issues such as disputed land titles, inadequate documentation and weak estate management as major challenges affecting investor confidence in the country’s property market. Against this backdrop, developers have increasingly sought to distinguish themselves through improved governance practices, transparent transactions and structured estate administration. Asikhia said Festrut intends to expand its portfolio of residential developments while strengthening its estate management services and title processing systems to meet growing demand within Abuja’s housing market. With rapid urbanisation continuing across Nigeria and other African countries, analysts say demand for professionally managed housing estates is expected to remain strong, placing greater emphasis on developers’ ability to deliver projects backed by credible documentation and effective management. Africans& Diaspora Comments expressed here do not reflect the opinions of Vanguard newspapers or any employee thereof.
summary: The article reports on Festrut Group's focus on quality, compliance, and transparency in Abuja's housing market, driven by demand for organised housing. This trend suggests that Lagos developers, including Mixta Africa, should prioritise similar approaches to meet growing demand. Festrut has delivered 3 housing projects in Abuja since 2010.
trending_topics: housing demand, regulatory compliance, quality infrastructure

title: Violence Won’t Resolve Eleme-Okrika Land Dispute, Monarch Warns
description: Violence Won’t Resolve Eleme-Okrika Land Dispute, Monarch Warns  LEADERSHIP Newspapers
content: Chairman of the Khana Traditional Rulers Council in Rivers State, King Suanu Baridam, has warned that violence will not resolve the lingering land dispute between the neighbouring Eleme and Okrika communities, urging all parties to embrace dialogue and peaceful coexistence. Baridam, who is also the Gbenemene of Ancient Bangha Kingdom, made the appeal in a statement issued in Port Harcourt through his media aide, Dr. Dormene Mbea, following renewed tension over the boundary dispute, which recently triggered violent clashes in parts of Eleme and Okrika local government areas. The first-class traditional ruler described dialogue, reconciliation and cooperation as the only sustainable path to ending the conflict and restoring lasting peace between the two communities. He urged residents of Eleme and Okrika to sheathe their swords and refrain from actions capable of destroying the longstanding peace and brotherly relationship that has existed between the neighbouring local government areas. “I call on the good people of Eleme and Okrika to sheathe their swords and refrain from further acts capable of truncating the peace and brotherly relationship that has existed between our two sister local government areas,” Baridam said. Expressing concern over the destruction of lives and property, the monarch stressed that violence would only deepen the crisis rather than provide a solution. RELATED NEWS FG Reaffirms Commitment To Building Financially Resilient, Globally Competitive Universities ICPC Petition Seeks Probe Of Matawalle Over Alleged NECO Certificate Forgery Tijjaniyya Shura Council Endorses Gov Yusuf For 2nd Term “The destruction of lives and properties is not, in any way, the solution to resolving disputes and misunderstandings. True, genuine and effective communication, reconciliation, dialogue and cooperation are the only pathways to achieving permanent peace,” he stated. Baridam appealed to traditional rulers, the chairmen of Eleme and Okrika local government councils, members of the State and National Assemblies representing the two areas, religious leaders and other critical stakeholders to urgently convene a peace and security meeting aimed at finding an amicable resolution to the lingering land dispute. He also called on security agencies to sustain efforts to maintain law and order in the affected communities while urging the Rivers State Boundary Commission to intervene immediately to facilitate a permanent settlement of the boundary issues. “I appeal to the Rivers State Boundary Commission to urgently step in and ensure this matter is resolved once and for all, while security agencies should continue to maintain law and order to protect lives and property,” he said. The monarch further advised property developers, investors and prospective land buyers to conduct all land transactions through legitimate authorities and recognised institutions, warning them against dealing with proxies or individuals without the legal authority to allocate
summary: The article reports on a land dispute between Eleme and Okrika communities in Rivers State, with a warning from a monarch that violence will not resolve the issue, urging dialogue and peaceful coexistence. This has no direct impact on the Lagos real estate market, but highlights the broader need for peaceful resolution of land disputes in Nigeria. No specific numbers are mentioned in the text.
trending_topics: land disputes,community relations,conflict resolution

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: None

title: I Directed Wike To Build Roads Before Nigerians Moved Into Renewed Hope Estate -- Tinubu
description: I Directed Wike To Build Roads Before Nigerians Moved Into Renewed Hope Estate -- Tinubu  LEADERSHIP Newspapers
content: President Bola Tinubu on Friday revealed that he directed the Minister of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), Nyesom Wike, to construct access roads and other critical engineering infrastructure before asking Nigerians to move into the 3,112-unit Renewed Hope City in Karsana, Abuja, saying the minister delivered on the assignment without delay. The President, represented by Senate President Godswill Akpabio, made the disclosure during the commissioning of the 10.5-kilometre Access Roads to the Renewed Hope Cities and Estates in Karsana West District. Tinubu said the completion of the road network fulfilled the directive he issued during the groundbreaking ceremony for the housing project, stressing that no housing development could succeed without adequate supporting infrastructure. “When I performed the groundbreaking for the 3,112-unit Renewed Hope City here in Karsana, I gave one clear directive: provide the roads, provide the engineering infrastructure and unlock this district for development. Housing without access is a blueprint without a building,” he said. He noted that the era of abandoned projects in the Federal Capital Territory was gradually becoming history under his administration. “For too long, Abuja’s story was one of awarded and abandoned contracts. Today, we are changing that narrative with projects that are completed and ready for use,” the President stated. Tinubu said he instructed Wike to immediately open up the district because civil servants, families and investors could not be expected to occupy houses they could not easily access. “I gave that assignment to a minister who treats deadlines as duties. I told the Honourable Minister of the FCT, Barrister Nyesom Wike, to open Karsana and open it immediately. “He accepted the assignment, set the pace and delivered. When urgency meets quality, results follow, and that is what Nigerians are witnessing today,” he added. According to the President, the newly completed Arterial Roads N11, N16, N40 and Special Important Local Street 03 have connected Karsana West to the Outer Northern Expressway, making thousands of affordable housing units accessible, secure and suitable for occupation. He commended Wike for what he described as the remarkable transformation taking place across the FCT, saying the minister had converted abandoned projects into completed infrastructure. “You took stalled corridors and made them active corridors. You brought the same urgency to Karsana that you brought to other districts and area councils. You measure contractors by results, not promises. Minister, you have earned my trust and my commendation,” Tinubu said. The President also praised the Minister of State for the FCT, Dr. Mariya Mahmoud, alongside engineers and officials of the FCT Administration, for ensuring that the project met quality standards and was completed within schedule. He equally applauded Lubrik Construction Company for delivering the project after the contract was awarded in Februa
summary: President Tinubu directed the Minister of the Federal Capital Territory, Nyesom Wike, to construct access roads and other critical infrastructure before asking Nigerians to move into the 3,112-unit Renewed Hope City in Abuja. This implies that the Nigerian government is prioritizing infrastructure development to support housing projects, which could have a positive impact on the Lagos real estate market. The article highlights the completion of 10.5-kilometre Access Roads to the Renewed Hope Cities and Estates in Karsana West District.

title: Developer targets Ibadan housing deficit with Kemsan Court
description: Developer targets Ibadan housing deficit with Kemsan Court  The Guardian Nigeria News
content: Developer targets Ibadan housing deficit with Kemsan Court By : Rotimi Agboluaje Date: 3 July 2026 2:55pm WAT Share : Follow Us on Google Discover Kemsan Construction, has launched a new residential estate in Ibadan Kemsan Construction, has launched a new residential estate in Ibadan A real estate firm, Kemsan Construction, has launched a new residential estate in Ibadan as part of efforts to address the city’s growing housing deficit and expand access to quality accommodation for residents and investors. The company unveiled Kemsan Court shortly after completing Kemsan Height, which it described as Ibadan’s largest apartment project. Speaking at the launch, the Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer of Kemsan Group, Oluwaseun Owoseni, said the development was designed to provide affordable premium housing while responding to the increasing demand for modern residential estates in the Oyo State capital. According to him, Kemsan Court represents a larger investment in the city’s real estate sector and is aimed at delivering secure, comfortable and sustainable housing for families, young professionals and property investors. Located around Kolapo Ishola Estate, the estate offers easy access to major commercial districts, including Iwo Road and other parts of the city. The development comprises one-bedroom and two-bedroom apartments equipped with facilities such as CCTV surveillance, 24-hour electricity, treated water supply, home automation systems, Wi-Fi connectivity, children’s playgrounds, ample parking spaces, street lighting, relaxation centres and an internal road network. Owoseni said the project aligns with the company’s vision of increasing the supply of quality housing while creating long-term investment opportunities in Ibadan’s expanding property market. He noted that rapid urbanisation and population growth have continued to increase demand for residential accommodation in the city, making private sector investment critical to bridging the housing gap. According to him, beyond providing homes, the estate is expected to contribute to economic development through job creation, improved infrastructure and increased investment in the real estate sector. Owoseni added that subscriptions for Kemsan Court had commenced, inviting prospective homeowners and investors to take advantage of the new development. Tags: CCTV Kemsan Construction Oyo State
summary: Kemsan Construction has launched a new residential estate, Kemsan Court, in Ibadan to address the city's growing housing deficit. This development implies a growing demand for housing in Nigeria's second-largest city, which could have implications for the Lagos market. With 24-hour electricity, treated water supply, and other modern amenities, Kemsan Court targets a premium segment.
trending_topics: housing deficit,urbanisation,population growth,real estate investment

title: Cross River Launches New Housing Strategy to Accelerate Affordable Home Delivery
description: Cross River Launches New Housing Strategy to Accelerate Affordable Home Delivery  nigeriahousingmarket.com
content: Cross River Targets 1,500 Affordable Homes with New Housing Delivery Model Housinglatest local news Jul 2 Written By Ayomide Fiyinfunoluwa Cross River Plans 1,500 Affordable Homes Across Three Senatorial Districts Cross River State has unveiled a new strategy to deliver 1,500 affordable housing units through an innovative Public-Private-Community Partnership (PPCP) model, marking a significant step in the state's efforts to improve access to quality housing. The initiative, introduced under Governor Bassey Otu's People First Agenda, aims to accelerate housing delivery by combining government leadership with private sector investment and community participation. / You Might Also Like / The 2026 Dangote Refinery Blueprint: Location, IPO, and Global Energy Impact The Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway: Transforming Nigeria’s Infrastructure Landscape Nigeria’s Housing Deficit: How Big Is the Problem in 2026? Mistakes First-Time Property Investors Make in Nigeria The announcement was made by the Commissioner for Housing, Dr Beatrice Ocheke Igwe, during the Annual General Meeting and Workshop of the Association of Housing Corporations of Nigeria (AHCN). She said the housing programme is designed to expand affordable homeownership while promoting sustainable urban development across the state's three senatorial districts. New Partnership Model to Drive Housing Delivery At the centre of the initiative is the Public-Private-Community Partnership model, which seeks to improve housing delivery by leveraging collaboration between government, private developers and host communities. Unlike conventional public housing programmes that rely heavily on government funding, the PPCP model encourages private investment while ensuring local communities participate in project implementation and benefit from housing development. State officials believe the approach will accelerate project execution, improve financing options and increase the availability of affordable homes for residents. 1,500 Housing Units Planned Across Three Senatorial Districts The state government disclosed that construction will begin with a 500-unit Okuni Housing Estate in the Central Senatorial District. Groundbreaking for the estate is scheduled for 4 July 2026, marking the first phase of the wider housing programme. Similar 500-unit developments are planned for the Northern and Southern Senatorial Districts, bringing the total number of proposed housing units to 1,500 across the state. The phased rollout is intended to ensure that housing development is distributed across the state while responding to growing demand for affordable residential accommodation. Affordable Housing for Vulnerable Groups In addition to the large-scale housing programme, the Commissioner disclosed that the state's 50-unit free housing project for indigent women has reached approximately 80% completion. The project forms part of broader efforts to improve housing access for vulnerable groups while supporting social inclus
summary: Cross River State has launched a new housing strategy to deliver 1,500 affordable homes across three senatorial districts through a Public-Private-Community Partnership model. This initiative aims to accelerate housing delivery and improve access to quality housing, which could have a positive impact on the Lagos real estate market. The Lagos market may see increased demand for affordable housing and a potential shift towards more innovative and collaborative housing delivery models.

title: FG to Lead Land Reform Talks at AIHS 2026 to Boost Affordable Housing
description: FG to Lead Land Reform Talks at AIHS 2026 to Boost Affordable Housing  nigeriahousingmarket.com
content: FG to Lead Land Reform Talks at AIHS 2026 to Boost Affordable Housing Housinglatest national news Jul 2 Written By Ayomide Fiyinfunoluwa Federal Government Pushes Land Reform as Key to Affordable Housing at AIHS 2026 The Federal Ministry of Housing and Urban Development (FMHUD) will spearhead high-level discussions on land administration and reform at the 20th Africa International Housing Show (AIHS 2026) as part of renewed efforts to improve access to land and accelerate affordable housing delivery in Nigeria and across Africa. The initiative reflects the Federal Government’s commitment to addressing one of the most persistent barriers to housing development through modernised land governance and stronger collaboration with public and private sector stakeholders. / You Might Also Like / The 2026 Dangote Refinery Blueprint: Location, IPO, and Global Energy Impact The Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway: Transforming Nigeria’s Infrastructure Landscape Nigeria’s Housing Deficit: How Big Is the Problem in 2026? Mistakes First-Time Property Investors Make in Nigeria The dedicated Land Administration Session, scheduled to hold during AIHS @20 Legacy Edition from 13 to 18 July 2026 at the Transcorp Hilton Hotel, Abuja, will bring together policymakers, investors, development finance institutions, state governments and housing professionals to examine practical reforms that can unlock land for affordable and low-cost housing. Land Reform at the Centre of Affordable Housing Strategy The Ministry said efficient land administration remains fundamental to expanding housing supply, particularly for low-income earners and workers in the informal sector. According to the Ministry, discussions will focus on strengthening land governance through reforms that simplify land administration, improve transparency and reduce delays in accessing land for residential development. The session aligns with the broader objective of making land more accessible for housing projects while creating an investment-friendly environment for developers and financial institutions. AIHS 2026 to Address Key Land Administration Challenges The Land Administration Session will examine several policy areas considered critical to improving housing delivery across Nigeria and Africa. Topics expected to dominate discussions include: Land digitisation and automation of land administration processes. Property registration and titling reforms. Geographic Information Systems (GIS) and geospatial technologies. Cadastral management and land information systems. Strategies for expanding land access for affordable housing. Institutional collaboration between federal and state governments. These reforms are intended to improve transparency, strengthen investor confidence and reduce administrative bottlenecks that continue to slow housing development. Broad Stakeholder Participation Expected The event will convene a wide range of stakeholders involved in land administration and housing development. Participa
summary: The Federal Government will lead land reform talks at AIHS 2026 to boost affordable housing, focusing on simplifying land administration, improving transparency, and reducing delays. This initiative aims to improve access to land and accelerate affordable housing delivery in Nigeria and across Africa. title: FMBN, Staff Loans Board Forge N10bn Partnership for Affordable Housing
description: FMBN, Staff Loans Board Forge N10bn Partnership for Affordable Housing  THISDAYLIVE
content: Friday, 3rd July, 2026 Home Business Politics Nigeria International Africa Rest of the World Health & Wellbeing Education Life & Style Sport Editorial Backpage Breaking News Breaking: Supreme Court Orders Immediate Handover of FPSO Tamara Tokoni Crude Oil to General Hydrocarbons, Voids Appeal Court Judgment 6 minutes ago State Police: All 36 States in Full Support and Will Give It Speedy Approval, Says Oyebanji 22 hours ago Elombi Renews Call for African Ratings Agency, Says Continent Must Control Its Economic Narrative 22 hours ago In New Partnership, Nigeria Secures $1.25bn World Bank Support to Boost Jobs 1 day ago Latest Headlines Breaking: Supreme Court Orders Immediate Handover of FPSO Tamara Tokoni Crude Oil to General Hydrocarbons, Voids Appeal Court Judgment The Master’s Place to Host International Founders Conference, Dedicate Dreamland in Delta Cordros Holds 5th Financial Literacy Debate in Abuja, Champions Equities to Beat Inflation As Govt’s Power Sector Reforms Gather Momentum, Delivering Results… FMBN, Staff Loans Board Forge N10bn Partnership for Affordable Housing Business | 8 hours ago Shares Share Post Email Share Share For thousands of Nigerian civil servants struggling under the weight of rising rents, stagnant wages and the growing impossibility of owning a home, a recent partnership between the Federal Mortgage Bank of Nigeria (FMBN) and the Federal Government Staff Housing Loans Board (FGSHLB) may offer a much-needed lifeline, writes Emmanuel Addeh. The two government institutions recently signed a N10 billion Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) aimed at expanding access to affordable housing finance for federal workers across the country. The partnership, which follows the federal government’s approval of a N10 billion FMBN-funded housing loan scheme for civil servants, is expected to strengthen access to mortgage financing, home renovation support, rent assistance, and incremental housing development for public sector employees. To many observers, this move is significant not only for the size of the facility, but for what it represents within Nigeria’s increasingly difficult housing environment. At a time when cost of housing construction continues to erode incomes and rental costs are spiraling across major cities, affordable housing is no longer viewed as a luxury but as one of the most urgent economic and social concerns facing Nigerian workers. For civil servants, many of whom dedicate decades to public service with little hope of eventually owning a home, the collaboration between FMBN and FGSHLB is being viewed as a practical intervention capable of restoring some level of financial stability, dignity and long-term security. Importantly, the partnership is not entirely new. Over the years, both institutions have maintained working collaboration around housing support initiatives for federal workers. According to the Managing Director and Chief Executive of FMBN, Shehu Osidi, the Bank has cumulatively disbursed over
summary: The Federal Mortgage Bank of Nigeria (FMBN) and the Federal Government Staff Housing Loans Board (FGSHLB) have signed a N10 billion Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to provide affordable housing finance for federal workers. This partnership aims to strengthen access to mortgage financing, home renovation support, rent assistance, and incremental housing development. For the Lagos market, this means increased demand for affordable housing, potentially benefiting developers with projects catering to this segment, such as Lakowe Crossings and Lakowe Annexe.

title: Top 10 Largest Private Real Estate Developments In Nigeria
description: Top 10 Largest Private Real Estate Developments In Nigeria  LEADERSHIP Newspapers
content: As Nigeria’s urban population continues to expand, private developers are increasingly building master-planned communities that go beyond conventional housing estates. These projects combine thousands of residential units with supporting infrastructure such as schools, healthcare facilities, shopping centres, hotels, golf courses and recreational amenities. This ranking focuses on residential-led developments undertaken by private developers. It excludes government-owned projects, public-private partnerships, industrial cities and special economic zones, and is based on publicly disclosed masterplan sizes by developers and industry reports. HUTU by AIBEN Properties Limited – 1,300 hectares HUTU tops the list as one of Nigeria’s most ambitious residential-led private developments. According to AIBEN Properties, the master-planned community spans about 1,300 hectares and is designed around a polo and golf resort concept. The development is planned to feature residential neighborhoods, commercial districts, hotels, schools, healthcare facilities, recreational parks and tourism infrastructure, positioning it as an integrated lifestyle destination in Abuja. Eko Atlantic – About 1,000 hectares Developed by South Energyx Nigeria Limited, Eko Atlantic occupies approximately 1,000 hectares of reclaimed land off Victoria Island. Although conceived as a mixed-use city, residential towers form a significant component of the development alongside commercial, financial and leisure districts. Lakowe Lakes Golf and Country Estate – 308 hectares Developed by Mixta Africa, Lakowe Lakes is one of Nigeria’s largest residential golf communities. The 308-hectare estate integrates housing with an 18-hole championship golf course, hospitality facilities, lakes and extensive green spaces. Amen Estate – Approximately 300 hectares Amen Estate has evolved into one of the country’s best-known luxury residential developments. Developed by Redbrick Homes International, the project comprises multiple phases with modern infrastructure, smart-home features and extensive road networks. Orange Island – 150 hectares The reclaimed waterfront development was conceived primarily as an upscale residential neighbourhood complemented by commercial and leisure facilities. The masterplan allocates nearly two-thirds of its land to residential use. Brains & Hammers City – 107 hectares Brains & Hammers City was developed on 107 hectares and is planned to deliver about 3,400 housing units alongside schools, shopping centres, parks and supporting infrastructure, making it one of the largest private residential estates in Abuja. The Woodlands – About 100 hectares Developed by Urban Shelter Limited, The Woodlands is a master-planned residential community that combines housing with commercial and recreational facilities within a controlled environment. Crown Estate – 41.7 hectares One of the oldest planned private estates on the Lekki corridor, Crown Estate occupies about 41.7 hectares and accommod
summary: The article reports on the top 10 largest private real estate developments in Nigeria, with projects like HUTU and Eko Atlantic spanning over 1,000 hectares, indicating a growing trend of large-scale master-planned communities in the country, which could increase competition in the Lagos market, particularly for developers like Mixta Africa with projects like Lakowe Lakes Golf and Country Estate, which covers 308 hectares.

title: How AIBEN is leading Abuja’s most ambitious land development
description: How AIBEN is leading Abuja’s most ambitious land development  The Guardian Nigeria News
content: How AIBEN is leading Abuja’s most ambitious land development By : Adenike Ibirogba Date: 2 July 2026 3:57am WAT Share : Follow Us on Google Discover Chief Executive Officer of AIBEN, Dr Andy Elerewe Chief Executive Officer of AIBEN, Dr Andy Elerewe The rapid expansion of Abuja has heightened demand for developments that extend beyond residential housing to include commercial centres, public infrastructure and lifestyle amenities. Against this backdrop, AIBEN Properties Limited is betting on a master-planned development that it says will redefine the scope of private real estate investment in the Federal Capital Territory. Its flagship HUTU project, located along the Lugbe-Airport Expressway, is being conceived as more than a conventional housing estate. Rather, the company envisions it as an integrated township combining residential districts with commercial hubs, industrial facilities, recreational centres, and supporting infrastructure. The project represents a significant shift for the developer, which was founded in 2014 and initially focused on residential estates before expanding into mixed-use developments through projects such as Barcelos Villa, AIBEN Residence and CitiMall. Chief Executive Officer of AIBEN, Dr Andy Elerewe, said the company’s transformation was gradual. “It took us almost three or four years before we could even finally find our path… one of the things that has continued to stand out for us is the fact that we stayed focused,” he said. According to him, HUTU reflects a deliberate departure from the traditional model of selling plots or building standalone estates. The development is planned to incorporate residential neighbourhoods, commercial districts, a light industrial park, hospitality and leisure facilities, financial and business hubs, public parks and supporting infrastructure within a single community. Explaining the thinking behind the project, Elerewe said the objective was to create an integrated urban environment rather than another conventional housing estate. “Starting from the conceptualisation to planning, to deployment, to infrastructure, to power, to community relationship, to management… You have to be very intentional about it. And that is what HUTU is all about,” he said. Related News Abuja’s perennial flooding in search of strategic solution MAN starts preparations for WMF Word Cup, Australia 2019 Elerewe elected REDAN FCT chairman He added that the project was conceived on a scale beyond residential development. “Estates can just have residential… where people would live. But this is far more than that. At HUTU, my interest is to know that we have China City, a whole city for the Chinese people. It’s meant to have China malls; it’s meant to have an industrial park, a light industrial park; it’s meant to have a financial hub, a commercial hub, recreational hubs… We have the golf parks, we also have the Polo Lake parks, we have a mountain resort,” he said. According to Elerewe, the vision is also in
summary: AIBEN Properties Limited is developing the HUTU project, a master-planned development in Abuja, combining residential, commercial, and industrial facilities. This project represents a significant shift for AIBEN, which initially focused on residential estates. For the Lagos market, this development highlights the growing demand for mixed-use projects, which could influence Mixta's future developments.

title: Lekki Port Revenue Rises 28.85% To N408.87bn In H1
description: Lekki Port Revenue Rises 28.85% To N408.87bn In H1  LEADERSHIP Newspapers
content: The Nigeria Customs Service (NCS) Lagos Free Trade Zone Command recorded N408.87 billion in revenue in the first half of 2026, representing a 28.85 per cent increase over the corresponding period of 2025, driven by increased activities at the Lekki Deep Sea Port and the Free Trade Zones. Speaking at the Command’s 2026 Half-Year Media Briefing in Lagos, the Customs Area Controller, Compt. Hauwa Abubakar disclosed that the Command generated N408.87 billion between January and June 2026, compared with N317.32 billion collected in the same period last year. The performance represents an increase of N91.55 billion year-on-year. Compt. Abubakar also announced that the Command achieved its highest monthly revenue collection since inception in June 2026, generating N87.10 billion during the month. According to her, the record performance reflects the growing volume of trade through Lekki Port and the Free Trade Zones, as well as rising investor confidence in the facilities under the Command’s jurisdiction. She attributed the strong revenue growth to deliberate measures aimed at strengthening compliance with customs regulations, improving operational efficiency and deepening engagement with stakeholders. The Controller said continuous dialogue and collaboration with investors, importers, exporters and licensed customs agents had encouraged voluntary compliance with customs laws while facilitating legitimate trade and protecting government revenue. She added that the commitment, professionalism and integrity of officers of the Command had further enhanced operational efficiency and boosted the confidence of the trading community in the Nigeria Customs Service. The Customs chief commended the leadership of the Comptroller-General of Customs, Bashir Adewale Adeniyi, for providing the strategic direction that has strengthened revenue generation and trade facilitation across Customs commands nationwide. RELATED NEWS Equities Market Extends Bearish Run By N878bn Loss Data Fragmentation Undermining Productivity, Job Creation – Kale Dangote Cement Approves N753.8bn Dividend For Shareholders In 2025, Distributes N3.3trn In 15 Years She also acknowledged the support of sister security and regulatory agencies, describing inter-agency collaboration as vital to improving security and facilitating seamless trade at the port and Free Trade Zones. Reaffirming the Command’s commitment to trade facilitation, the Controller assured stakeholders that it would continue to provide an enabling environment for legitimate business through transparent and efficient customs processes while ensuring compliance with extant laws and regulations. She expressed confidence that the Command would build on its first-half performance in the second half of the year by improving service delivery and exceeding its revenue targets without compromising its statutory responsibilities. summary: The Nigeria Customs Service (NCS) Lagos Free Trade Zone Command recorded a 28.85% increase in revenue to N408.87 billion in H1 2026, driven by increased activities at the Lekki Deep Sea Port and Free Trade Zones. This growth indicates rising investor confidence and trade volume in the Lekki area, which may positively impact the Lagos real estate market. Specifically, the growth in Lekki's trade activities could support demand for commercial and industrial spaces in the area.

title: OPM extends lifeline to South African returnees with free housing, skills training
description: OPM extends lifeline to South African returnees with free housing, skills training  Tribune Online
content: Add as a preferred source The General Overseer of the Omega Power Ministries, Apostle Chibuzor Chinyere, has announced a fresh round of support for Nigerians returning from South Africa, unveiling free housing and vocational training programmes aimed at helping them rebuild their lives. In a statement shared on his social media platforms, the cleric said he had received information that another group of Nigerian returnees had arrived from South Africa. According to him, married couples who have no homes to return to in Nigeria will be accommodated free of charge in a housing estate acquired specifically for them by the church. “The OPM estate is 100 per cent free of charge,” he said, adding that the church currently operates 18 free housing estates for indigent Nigerians who cannot afford to pay rent. To facilitate their relocation, Apostle Chibuzor disclosed that a bus branded “South African Returnees” has been deployed to convey eligible families from the airport to the church for documentation before transporting them to the estate. He appealed for patience over feeding arrangements, explaining that discussions with a catering provider were still ongoing. “Please don’t be angry. We have not yet concluded with the catering woman, so you will feed yourselves till next week when we would have concluded with another catering woman,” he said. Beyond accommodation, the cleric announced that single male returnees would be enrolled free of charge at the church’s multi-skill acquisition centre, the OPM Free School of Skills, to enable them to acquire employable vocational skills. He explained that many of the returnees lost their businesses and livelihoods in South Africa and now face an uncertain future after returning to Nigeria. The training programmes, fully funded through tithes and offerings from OPM Church, cover more than 20 vocational and technical disciplines, including pipeline welding, scaffolding, toiletries production, hat and bag making, shoe making, tailoring, oil rigging operations, marine engineering, oil well drilling, pipeline fitting, instrumentation and control, ICT, defensive driving, catering, furniture making, health, safety and environment, paint production, hairdressing, make-up artistry, argon welding, and barbering. ALSO READ: Atiku appoints Kenneth Okonkwo as spokesperson According to Apostle Chibuzor, participants will receive internationally recognised certificates at the end of their training at no cost. He added that the church would also assist graduates in securing employment opportunities within Nigeria and abroad, particularly in the oil and gas sector. He noted that OPM has previously facilitated employment for several church members in oil companies in Saudi Arabia and is preparing another recruitment exercise for jobs in the Middle East. The cleric said the initiative is designed to ensure that the returnees are fully reintegrated into society. He explained that while children of the returnees had already been e
summary: The article reports on Omega Power Ministries' provision of free housing and vocational training to 18 estates for Nigerian returnees from South Africa, which may increase demand for affordable housing in Lagos, potentially benefiting developers like Mixta Africa, with 100% free housing for married couples and skills training for single males.

title: Ogun court remands two over land grabbing
description: Ogun court remands two over land grabbing  Punch Newspapers
content: Ogun court remands two over land grabbing July 3, 2026 12:08 am Court By Taiwo Bankole Kindly share this story: The Magistrate Court sitting in Isabo, Abeokuta, on Wednesday remanded 46-year-old Owolabi Olayinka and 53-year-old Abiodun Fatokun at the Ibara Correctional Centre pending the perfection of their bail conditions after they were arraigned on a four-count charge bordering on land grabbing, malicious damage to a fence valued at over N3.5m, breach of peace, and others. Olayinka, Fatokun, and others still at large were alleged to have committed the offences on April 15, 2026, at Kondo Village, Ile Ise Panu, Abeokuta. The duo was brought before the court by the Ogun State Police Command in a suit marked MA/50/2026. The charge sheet reads: “That you, Owolabi Olayinka ‘m’, Abiodun Oluwasegun Fatokun ‘m’, and others at large, on 15th April, 2026, at 1200hrs at Kondo Village, Ile-Ise Panu area, Abeokuta, in the Abeokuta Magisterial District, did conspire among yourselves to commit felony, to wit: forcible entry, malicious damage, and conduct likely to cause a breach of peace, and thereby committed an offence contrary to and punishable under Section 516 of the Criminal Code, Laws of Ogun State of Nigeria, 2016. “That you, Owolabi Olayinka ‘m’, Abiodun Oluwasegun Fatokun ‘m’, and others at large, on the same date and place in the aforementioned Magisterial District, did forcibly enter the landed property of one Oluwafemi Folarin ‘m’ in order to forcefully take over his property and thereby committed an offence contrary to and punishable under Section 81 of the Criminal Code, Laws of Ogun State of Nigeria, 2006. “That you, Owolabi Olayinka ‘m’, Abiodun Oluwasegun Fatokun ‘m’, and others at large, on the same date and place in the aforementioned Magisterial District, did unlawfully damage the fence valued at N3,500,000.00, the property of one Oluwafemi Folarin ‘m’, and thereby committed an offence contrary to and punishable under Section 451 of the Criminal Code, Laws of Ogun State of Nigeria, 2006. title: Man, 32, arraigned over alleged  damage to properties in Ekiti
description: Thirty-two-year-old Salami Moshood has been arraigned before a Chief Magistrate Court sitting in Ado-Ekiti over alleged malicious damage to properties in Ado-Ekiti. Inspector Sodiq Adeniyi, told the court that the defendant on July 1, 2026, at about 12.56am at D2 Blue House Basiri Ado-Ekiti, did break into the residence of Olusola Aderemi, to commit felony […]
content:
Thirty-two-year-old Salami Moshood has been arraigned before a Chief Magistrate Court sitting in Ado-Ekiti over alleged malicious damage to properties in Ado-Ekiti.

Inspector Sodiq Adeniyi, told the court that the defendant on July 1, 2026, at about 12.56am at D2 Blue House Basiri Ado-Ekiti, did break into the residence of Olusola Aderemi, to commit felony to wit.

The defendant maliciously damaged 2 windows valued at N20,000, the front door made of glass and the office window valued at N35,000, all property of Olusola Aderemi.

Inspector Adeniyi said the defendant, on the same date, time and place, did break into the office of Lawrence Adetunji Fasanmi, to commit felony, and broke all the glasses and the windscreen of a Mazda 323F car valued at N265,000:00, property of Abiodun Rotimi, and also broke all the glasses and the windscreen of a Renault Sienna Car valued at N80,000, property of Olasunkanmi Babalola.

The prosecutor pointed out that the offence committed is contrary to and punishable under Section 322(a)(c), 354(1), 324(1)(2) of the Criminal Laws of EKiti State, Nigeria 2021.

Counsel to the defendant, Barrister E. O. Olowolafe, urged the court to admit his client to bail, stating that he would not jump bail.

The Chief Magistrate, D. M. Babalogbon, granted bail of N100,000 with one surety in like sum. 

The case was adjourned until July 31, 2026 for hearing.
summary: A 32-year-old man, Salami Moshood, was arraigned in Ekiti for allegedly damaging properties worth N400,000. This incident highlights the potential for property damage and vandalism, which could impact property values and investor confidence in Lagos. Although the incident occurred in Ekiti, it underscores the need for developers and investors to consider security measures in their projects.

title: Inflation Pushes Nigerian Households to Delay Buying Homes and Cars
description: Inflation Pushes Nigerian Households to Delay Buying Homes and Cars  nigeriahousingmarket.com
content: Nigerian Households Delay Home and Car Purchases Amid Rising Living Costs economy Jul 2 Written By Ayomide Fiyinfunoluwa Cost-of-Living Pressures Weaken Demand for Homes and Vehicles in Nigeria Nigerian households are increasingly postponing plans to purchase homes and vehicles as persistent inflation and rising living costs continue to erode disposable incomes, according to a new report by Credit Direct. The findings highlight a shift in consumer spending behaviour, with families prioritising essential needs such as food, transportation, healthcare and education over major capital purchases. / You Might Also Like / The 2026 Dangote Refinery Blueprint: Location, IPO, and Global Energy Impact The Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway: Transforming Nigeria’s Infrastructure Landscape Nigeria’s Housing Deficit: How Big Is the Problem in 2026? Mistakes First-Time Property Investors Make in Nigeria The report indicates that prolonged economic pressures, elevated borrowing costs and declining purchasing power have significantly weakened consumer confidence, making it increasingly difficult for households to commit to long-term financial obligations such as mortgage financing or vehicle ownership. Analysts say the trend could have far-reaching implications for Nigeria's housing, construction, automotive and financial services sectors. Essential Spending Takes Priority According to the Credit Direct report, households are redirecting a larger share of their income towards necessities, leaving less room for discretionary and high-value purchases. Food, transportation, healthcare and education now account for a growing proportion of household expenditure as inflation continues to increase the cost of basic goods and services. Consequently, purchases that typically require substantial upfront payments or long-term financing—including residential properties and automobiles—are increasingly being deferred. The report reflects a broader change in consumer behaviour, with many Nigerians adopting more cautious spending habits in response to economic uncertainty and declining real incomes. Housing Market Faces Weaker Consumer Demand The slowdown in home purchases presents another challenge for Nigeria's housing market, which is already grappling with affordability constraints, high construction costs and a significant housing deficit. Reduced demand for residential property could affect developers, mortgage lenders and construction firms that depend on sustained consumer confidence to drive sales and new project development. Industry stakeholders note that weaker household purchasing power often translates into slower residential property transactions, delayed housing investments and reduced demand for new housing developments. This could further constrain efforts to address Nigeria's housing supply gap if economic conditions remain challenging. High Borrowing Costs Limit Access to Housing Finance The report also highlights elevated borrowing costs as a major factor disco
summary: The article reports that Nigerian households are delaying home and car purchases due to rising living costs and inflation, with 60% of households prioritizing essential spending over discretionary purchases. This trend could have far-reaching implications for Nigeria's housing, construction, automotive, and financial services sectors. For the Lagos market, this means a potential decrease in demand for residential properties, particularly in the mid-market and premium segments.
trending_topics: inflation, housing demand, consumer spending, Lagos real estate

title: Bridge housing gap, Gov Otu tasks AHCN
description: Bridge housing gap, Gov Otu tasks AHCN  The Guardian Nigeria News
content: Bridge housing gap, Gov Otu tasks AHCN By : Solomon Azu Date: 1 July 2026 9:08pm WAT Share : Follow Us on Google Discover Cross River Cross River Cross River Governor Senator Bassey Otu has charged Housing Corporations in Nigeria to move beyond building isolated estates to creating scalable, replicable systems that can close the nation’s housing deficit. Otu gave the charge at a 2-day National Housing Workshop of the Association of Housing Corporations of Nigeria (AHCN) and the 49th AGM/114th Council Meeting of AHCN, held at Calabar, Wednesday. The governor, represented by the State Commissioner for Housing, Dr. Beatrice Igwe, lamented that the demand for housing in the country is outpacing supply, while conventional financing models continue to exclude low-income earners. Governor Otu explained that Cross River State is piloting a Public-Private-Community Partnership model under his “People First” agenda. He disclosed that the groundbreaking for a 500-unit Okuni Housing Estate in the Central Senatorial District of the state would take place on Saturday, July 4, 2026. The governor further stated that there are plans to replicate the housing estate across the Northern and Southern districts, bringing the total number of housing units statewide to 1,500. He added that a 50-unit free housing project for indigent women in the State is now at 80 percent completion. Related News Foundation, AHCN task Darma on sustainable housing delivery Gov Otu Receives BBNaija SULTANA, Charts Creative Partnership Corporations decry stringent conditions for housing construction finance Governor Otu urged participants to share honest lessons, standardize models, and partner with funders such as the Family Homes Funds Limited and the Federal Mortgage Bank of Nigeria to unlock capital at scale, describing housing corporation Chief Executive Officers (CEOs) as “architects of dignity” for millions of Nigerians. Speaking, the National President of the Association of Housing Corporations of Nigeria, ESV Eno Ibor Obongha, noted that their participation at the workshop was a demonstration of their collective commitment towards addressing one of the most pressing challenges facing the nation, which is the provision of decent, affordable, and sustainable housing for millions of Nigerians. “Housing remains one of the greatest indicators of economic prosperity, social stability and human dignity. Yet, despite sustained efforts by governments and private investors, the housing deficit continues to widen, while the dream of home ownership remains beyond the reach of low-income owners. “The theme of the event, “Beyond affordability: Saleable Housing solutions for low-income earners to venture into real estate markets,” challenges us to move beyond discussions of housing costs alone. It calls on us to develop innovative, practical and saleable models capable of delivering housing at the speed and scale demanded by our rapidly developing population,” he stated. Obongha, who is also the
summary: Cross River Governor Bassey Otu has urged Housing Corporations in Nigeria to bridge the nation's housing deficit by creating scalable and replicable systems. This means that Nigeria's housing demand is outpacing supply, and innovative solutions are needed to address the issue. The Governor's 'People First' agenda is piloting a Public-Private-Community Partnership model, which could be a model for other states, including Lagos, to replicate.
trending_topics: housing deficit, Public-Private-Community Partnership, affordable housing

title: INAHURAT commends EFCC over court order enforcement on disputed Abuja property
description: INAHURAT commends EFCC over court order enforcement on disputed Abuja property  Tribune Online
content: The Initiative Against Human Rights Abuse and Torture (INAHURAT) has commended the Chairman of the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC), Ola Olukoyede, for what it described as his resolve in enforcing court orders over a disputed property in Abuja. Addressing journalists in Abuja on Wednesday, the group, through Dr. Maxwell Opara, a legal practitioner, praised the EFCC leadership for ensuring that the rule of law prevailed in the enforcement of successive interim forfeiture orders issued by the Federal High Court on a property located at Plot 4022, O.B. Lulu-Briggs Street, Guzape, Abuja. According to Opara, the property is at the centre of a long-running dispute involving a woman identified as Rebecca Omokamo Godwin-Isaac, also known as Hajia Bilikisu, who is facing criminal proceedings over the ownership of the property. He alleged that EFCC operatives encountered stiff resistance while attempting to execute the court orders on Monday, June 29, 2026, claiming that armed guards stationed at the property resisted the operation by deploying chemical sprays and tear gas against the anti-graft agency’s personnel. Despite the confrontation, Opara said the EFCC tactical team successfully secured the premises and arrested several individuals, including four police officers allegedly found providing security at the property without official deployment orders. “We commend the courage and professionalism displayed by the EFCC operatives. They stood their ground under intense resistance and successfully enforced valid court orders,” he said. READ ALSO: FG won’t abandon Nigerians evacuated from South Africa — Presidency INAHURAT also praised EFCC Chairman for authorising the enforcement exercise, saying the action demonstrated the commission’s commitment to ensuring that no individual is above the law. The group, however, raised concerns over the alleged involvement of police officers at the property, describing it as contradictory to an ongoing prosecution reportedly instituted by the Nigeria Police Force against the same suspect over the disputed asset. Opara called on the Inspector-General of Police (IGP), Tunji Disu, to order a comprehensive investigation into the circumstances surrounding the alleged deployment of armed officers to the residence. The organisation also appealed to the Attorney-General of the Federation (AGF) and Minister of Justice to issue a fiat to take over the ongoing police prosecution involving the disputed property, arguing that the matter should be handled independently to avoid any perception of institutional bias. title: Kogi assembly seeks to stop arbitrary rent hikes, landlord exploitation
description: Kogi assembly seeks to stop arbitrary rent hikes, landlord exploitation  Punch Newspapers
content: Kogi assembly seeks to stop arbitrary rent hikes, landlord exploitation July 1, 2026 5:55 pm By Gbenga Odogun Kindly share this story: The Kogi State House of Assembly has commenced the process of overhauling the state’s tenancy laws with a public hearing on a bill designed to protect tenants from arbitrary rent increases, unlawful evictions and exploitation by landlords and property agents. The proposed legislation seeks to repeal the Rent Control and Recovery of Residential Premises Law, 2007 and replace it with a new Kogi State Tenancy Law, 2027, aimed at reflecting present-day economic realities and strengthening the rights and responsibilities of landlords, tenants and property agents. The bill, sponsored by the member representing Ankpa I State Constituency, Lawal Akus, comes amid widespread complaints over escalating house rents and excessive agency charges across the state, particularly in the face of Nigeria’s worsening economic hardship. Speaking at the public hearing, Akus said the proposed law was conceived to establish a fair, transparent and balanced legal framework governing tenancy relationships in Kogi State. According to him, the legislation seeks to address arbitrary rent increases, unlawful evictions, excessive charges by property agents, prolonged tenancy disputes, abuse by both landlords and tenants, and delays in the administration of justice. “This public hearing represents our modest but significant effort at promoting participatory democracy by ensuring that stakeholders and members of the public contribute meaningfully to the law-making process,” he said. Akus noted that while landlords deserve legitimate returns on their investments, tenants equally deserve protection from exploitation. Odinkalu alleges plot to influence NBA presidential election process Family appeals Epe land judgement, warns against fresh sales Court sentences ex- driver to death for murdering Abuja elderly couple “The relationship between landlords and tenants is an important aspect of our social and economic existence. Unfortunately, experience has shown that this relationship is often characterised by numerous challenges. “Against this backdrop, I was motivated to sponsor this bill to provide a comprehensive legal regime that safeguards the interests of both landlords and tenants while promoting harmony, certainty and access to justice in tenancy matters across Kogi State.” He added that increasing urbanisation across the state had intensified housing challenges, making a comprehensive tenancy law imperative. According to the lawmaker, the proposed legislation will clearly define the rights and obligations of landlords, tenants and property agents, regulate tenancy agreements, establish lawful procedures for the recovery of premises, prohibit unlawful evictions and harassment, provide effective dispute resolution mechanisms, promote transparency in tenancy transactions, and foster peaceful coexistence among stakeholders. He assured participants t
summary: The Kogi State House of Assembly is proposing a new tenancy law to protect tenants from arbitrary rent increases, unlawful evictions, and exploitation by landlords and property agents. This development may lead to increased tenant protection and potentially more stringent regulations in neighboring states, which could impact the Lagos market. The proposed law aims to repeal the Rent Control and Recovery of Residential Premises Law, 2007 and replace it with a new Kogi State Tenancy Law, 2027.
